title: Implement Source Schema Discovery from Database Connections and Schema Files
body:
## User Story

As a data engineer, I want Concoction to discover schema metadata from supported databases and schema files so that I can generate synthetic data without manually recreating models.

## Business Requirements

- Support onboarding from at least PostgreSQL and SQLite in MVP.
- Support schema file import (DDL or JSON schema representation) for offline workflows.
- Produce a consistent discovery output regardless of source.
- Fail with actionable errors when source metadata is incomplete or inaccessible.

## Technical Requirements

- Implement provider adapters behind `ISchemaProvider`.
- Capture: schema name, table name, columns, nullability, PK/FK, defaults, uniqueness/index metadata.
- Normalize provider-specific types into canonical intermediate schema model.
- Add discovery diagnostics payload (warnings/errors per object).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Given a valid PostgreSQL connection, discovery returns all tables/columns/FKs in canonical model.
- [ ] Given a valid SQLite connection, discovery returns canonical model with equivalent fidelity.
- [ ] Given a supported schema file, discovery output matches canonical model contract.
- [ ] Discovery surfaces non-fatal warnings (e.g., computed columns unsupported) without crashing.
- [ ] Unit + integration tests validate provider behavior and error paths.

title: Build Canonical Schema Graph with Dependency and Cycle Classification
body:
## User Story

As a platform engineer, I want schema relationships represented as a dependency graph so that generation order and integrity logic can be computed automatically.

## Business Requirements

- Represent parent-child dependencies for all FK edges.
- Detect and classify cycles, self-references, and bridge-table patterns.
- Expose graph metadata for planning and validation steps.

## Technical Requirements

- Implement graph builder over discovered schema.
- Add cycle detection and strongly-connected-component classification.
- Emit insertion batches for acyclic components.
- Persist graph diagnostics in run metadata.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Graph includes every FK edge discovered from source schema.
- [ ] Self-referencing tables are flagged distinctly from multi-table cycles.
- [ ] Acyclic schemas produce deterministic insertion order.
- [ ] Cyclic components are identified and returned with remediation flags.
- [ ] Tests cover acyclic, self-reference, and multi-node cycle scenarios.

title: Implement SQL-Type-to-DataKind Mapping and Generator Strategy Resolution
body:
## User Story

As a developer, I want automatic mapping from source SQL types to generator strategies so that data generation is accurate and requires minimal manual setup.

## Business Requirements

- Common SQL types must map predictably to canonical `DataKind`.
- Unsupported/ambiguous types must degrade gracefully with warnings.
- Strategy selection must be overridable via rules.

## Technical Requirements

- Extend `SqlTypeMapper` for PostgreSQL + SQLite MVP coverage.
- Register default generator strategies in `GeneratorRegistry`.
- Add resolution precedence: explicit rule > inferred strategy > fallback default.
- Return mapping diagnostics for observability.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Supported SQL types map to expected `DataKind` values.
- [ ] Ambiguous types produce warning + safe fallback.
- [ ] Explicit column rule overrides inferred strategy.
- [ ] Registry resolves by `DataKind` and optional strategy key.
- [ ] Tests validate mapping matrix and override precedence.

title: Add Deterministic Seeding and Reproducible Run Execution
body:
## User Story

As a QA lead, I want generation runs to be reproducible from a seed so that test failures can be replayed exactly.

## Business Requirements

- Same schema + same seed + same config must produce identical output.
- Different seeds must produce distinct data while preserving constraints.
- Seed provenance must be stored in run metadata.

## Technical Requirements

- Standardize deterministic RNG through `IRandomService`.
- Thread seed scope through table, column, and row contexts.
- Store run seed, config hash, and generator version in run summary.
- Ensure parallel execution does not break determinism.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Re-running with same inputs yields byte-equivalent export output.
- [ ] Changing seed changes values while preserving structural validity.
- [ ] Run summary includes seed and reproducibility metadata.
- [ ] Determinism remains intact under parallelized table batches.
- [ ] Automated tests prove replay behavior.

title: Implement Referential Integrity Planner with Cycle-Safe Materialization
body:
## User Story

As a data engineer, I want Concoction to maintain referential integrity across generated datasets so that downstream systems behave like production.

## Business Requirements

- No orphan FK references in final dataset.
- Support acyclic and cyclic schemas in MVP.
- Respect nullable vs required foreign keys.

## Technical Requirements

- Use dependency graph to plan insertion order.
- Implement two-phase materialization for cycles (insert placeholders + backfill).
- Maintain key pools for parent reference sampling.
- Validate FK existence before export.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Acyclic schemas generate with zero FK violations.
- [ ] Cyclic schemas complete using configured backfill strategy.
- [ ] Non-nullable FKs are always populated with valid references.
- [ ] Nullable FKs follow configured null-rate rules.
- [ ] Integrity tests pass on representative multi-table fixtures.

title: Deliver Structured Relational Data Generation Engine with Constraint Awareness
body:
## User Story

As an application tester, I want realistic structured synthetic rows that respect schema constraints so that I can run integration tests safely.

## Business Requirements

- Generate data for all supported primitive and date/time kinds in MVP.
- Respect nullability, basic uniqueness, and value range constraints.
- Allow table-level target row counts.

## Technical Requirements

- Implement row materialization pipeline via `IRowMaterializer`.
- Enforce uniqueness via per-column/index trackers.
- Add configurable default distributions (categorical, numeric ranges, temporal windows).
- Add failure reporting for unsatisfied constraints.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Generated rows satisfy nullability and type constraints.
- [ ] Uniqueness constraints are met within configured thresholds.
- [ ] Row counts per table align with requested plan.
- [ ] Generation failures surface actionable diagnostics.
- [ ] Unit tests cover constraints and edge cases.

title: Build Sensitive Field Policy Engine for GDPR/HIPAA-Oriented Safety
body:
## User Story

As a compliance officer, I want sensitive columns to be automatically detected and transformed so that synthetic outputs do not expose regulated data patterns.

## Business Requirements

- Identify common sensitive fields (name, email, phone, address, MRN/SSN-like identifiers).
- Enforce approved strategies (mask, hash, fake, null, preserve-format synthetic).
- Deny unsafe strategy combinations by policy.

## Technical Requirements

- Extend `ISensitiveFieldPolicy` with policy evaluation + enforcement result.
- Add configurable keyword/metadata-based field classification.
- Add policy profiles (default, strict healthcare, strict finance).
- Log policy decisions without logging raw sensitive values.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sensitive fields are classified with explainable rationale.
- [ ] Disallowed strategies are blocked with explicit errors.
- [ ] Approved strategies produce compliant synthetic values.
- [ ] Policy profile selection changes enforcement behavior as expected.
- [ ] Tests cover positive/negative compliance paths.

title: Implement Rule Configuration for Global/Table/Column Overrides
body:
## User Story

As a data steward, I want to define generation rules at multiple scopes so that synthetic data matches domain expectations and testing scenarios.

## Business Requirements

- Support precedence across global, table, and column scopes.
- Allow overrides for distributions, locale, min/max, null-rate, and strategy.
- Validate rule files before execution.

## Technical Requirements

- Extend `RuleConfigurationService` validation and merge semantics.
- Add schema-aware rule validation (reject unknown table/column references).
- Support deterministic serialization of effective rules into run artifact.
- Emit warnings for ignored/invalid rule fragments.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Effective rules respect documented precedence order.
- [ ] Invalid references fail fast before generation begins.
- [ ] Effective merged configuration is persisted per run.
- [ ] Rule changes alter generation output predictably.
- [ ] Tests cover merge/validation/error behavior.

title: Add JSON and Unstructured Field Synthesis with Schema-Constrained Outputs
body:
## User Story

As a product team member, I want realistic JSON and text-rich synthetic content so that API and document-heavy features can be tested end-to-end.

## Business Requirements

- Support JSON column generation with nested structures.
- Support controlled unstructured text generation for selected fields.
- Enforce safety filters and output constraints.

## Technical Requirements

- Add JSON template/schema-based generators.
- Add prompt-template semantic generator adapter with guardrails (length, banned terms, schema validation).
- Provide fallback deterministic generator when semantic generation is disabled/unavailable.
- Include per-field generation provenance in run metadata.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] JSON fields validate against configured schema/template.
- [ ] Unstructured text respects length/style/policy constraints.
- [ ] Semantic generator failures degrade gracefully to deterministic fallback.
- [ ] Generated content is traceable to strategy used.
- [ ] Tests validate both guarded semantic and fallback paths.

title: Create Dataset Validation Suite for Integrity, Quality, and Distribution Checks
body:
## User Story

As a release engineer, I want automated post-generation validation so that only high-quality synthetic datasets are exported.

## Business Requirements

- Validate FK integrity, nullability, uniqueness, and type conformance.
- Provide configurable threshold checks for distribution drift.
- Produce machine-readable and human-readable validation reports.

## Technical Requirements

- Extend `IConstraintEvaluator` to support table and cross-table rules.
- Add validation report contract with severity levels.
- Add CLI `validate` output in JSON + summary text.
- Gate export on validation policy (fail-on-error, warn-only modes).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Validation catches FK, nullability, and uniqueness violations.
- [ ] Distribution checks report pass/fail against configured thresholds.
- [ ] Report includes issue location, severity, and remediation hint.
- [ ] Export is blocked when policy requires zero critical issues.
- [ ] Tests cover success, warning, and fail gating.

title: Implement Export Adapters for SQL, CSV, and JSON with Run Manifest
body:
## User Story

As a developer, I want flexible export formats so that I can load synthetic data into databases, pipelines, and test fixtures quickly.

## Business Requirements

- Support SQL seed scripts, CSV files, and JSON bundles in MVP.
- Ensure exported artifacts are consistent with generated dataset and schema.
- Produce a run manifest for traceability and replay.

## Technical Requirements

- Implement/extend `IExporter` adapters for SQL/CSV/JSON.
- Preserve insertion order constraints in SQL output.
- Include schema version, seed, row counts, and checksum in manifest.
- Support deterministic file naming and folder structure.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SQL exports can be applied without FK/constraint failure on target DB.
- [ ] CSV/JSON exports contain expected rows and column ordering.
- [ ] Manifest includes required reproducibility fields and checksums.
- [ ] Exporters are selectable from CLI.
- [ ] Integration tests validate round-trip usability.

title: Build Run Orchestration, Progress Reporting, and Auditable Lineage
body:
## User Story

As a platform operator, I want generation runs to be observable and auditable so that I can troubleshoot failures and demonstrate control to stakeholders.

## Business Requirements

- Track run lifecycle states (queued, running, completed, failed, cancelled).
- Expose progress metrics and per-step status.
- Persist lineage metadata for who/what/when/how a dataset was generated.

## Technical Requirements

- Add orchestration state model and run repository.
- Emit structured telemetry (trace/span/metrics) with a single `FabricationRunId`.
- Persist tool/step decisions, effective config hash, exporter outputs, validation results.
- Add CLI progress output and final run summary artifact.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every run has unique ID and complete lifecycle record.
- [ ] Progress can be queried while job is executing.
- [ ] Failure reports include failed step, error class, and retry context.
- [ ] Final summary links schema source, seed, rules, validation, and exports.
- [ ] Tests verify state transitions and lineage persistence.
